title: Room One: Domestic Objects
description: Everyday items that shape our daily lives and reflect cultural values

artifacts.title: Ceramic Bowl
artifacts.description: A hand-thrown ceramic bowl representing the craftsmanship and utility of mid-century domestic pottery. This piece reflects the intersection of functional design and artistic expression.

artifacts.title: Brass Scale
artifacts.description: A vintage kitchen scale used for measuring ingredients. This object represents the rationalization of domestic labor and the importance of precision in cooking.

artifacts.title: Linen Napkin
artifacts.description: Hand-embroidered linen napkin from the early 20th century. The embroidered details reflect domestic pride and the value placed on table presentation.

artifacts.title: Glass Bottle
artifacts.description: An amber glass bottle likely used for storing liquids or preserves. This functional object demonstrates the evolution of glass manufacturing and domestic storage.

title: Room Two: Textiles & Fashion
description: Garments and fabrics that express identity, cultural tradition, and personal style

artifacts.title: Wool Scarf
artifacts.description: A hand-woven wool scarf featuring traditional geometric patterns. The textile demonstrates both cultural heritage and individual expression through pattern and color.

artifacts.title: Embroidered Patch
artifacts.description: A decorative embroidered patch representing subcultural identity and personal adornment. These objects became symbols of belonging to communities and movements.

artifacts.title: Silk Ribbon
artifacts.description: A finely woven silk ribbon from the Victorian era. The delicate material speaks to changing notions of femininity and the importance of ornamentation in daily dress.

artifacts.title: Leather Belt
artifacts.description: A worn leather belt with a distinctive metal buckle, showing signs of regular use. The object tells a story of time passing and personal history through its patina.

title: Room Three: Tools & Labor
description: Implements of work that reflect human ingenuity and the history of craft

artifacts.title: Hand Chisel
artifacts.description: A well-used woodworking chisel with a handle worn smooth by years of use. This tool connects us to generations of craftspeople and their intimate knowledge of materials.

artifacts.title: Sewing Thimble
artifacts.description: A small metal thimble used in hand sewing. This humble object represents the invisible labor and skill of needle work that was essential to garment production and repair.

artifacts.title: Gardening Fork
artifacts.description: A sturdy iron fork used for gardening, showing wear from decades of turning soil. The tool represents the connection between humans and the earth.

artifacts.title: Measuring Tape
artifacts.description: A cloth measuring tape used in tailoring. This tool represents precision and the standardization of measurements in craft and manufacturing.
